title: Climate vs Growth
subtitle: The targets are binding. The factories are hiring.

turns.prompt: International treaty deadlines arrive. Emissions must fall 40% this decade — or face trade penalties.

choices.label: Enforce a strict emissions cap immediately
choices.reaction: Smokestacks idle. Workers march in industrial towns.

choices.label: Negotiate a delay to protect jobs

delayed.headline: Treaty partners signal carbon border tariffs in retaliation

choices.reaction: Factories keep running. Diplomats warn of sanctions.

choices.label: Carbon tax with rebates for low earners
choices.reaction: Fuel prices rise. Rebate cheques soften the blow — for some.

turns.prompt: Early data shows emissions dipping — or stalling. Both sides claim vindication.

choices.label: Fund a green jobs transition programme
choices.reaction: Solar installers hire. Coal towns watch warily — then vote.

choices.label: Buy carbon credits abroad instead of cutting at home
choices.flavour: Pay others to pollute less for you.
choices.reaction: Spreadsheets show compliance. Activists call it cheating.

choices.label: Cut environmental rules to boost output

delayed.headline: Lawsuits and clean-up costs eat into the growth dividend

choices.reaction: GDP ticks up. Hospital admissions for respiratory illness follow.

turns.prompt: Extreme weather destroys infrastructure. Insurers raise premiums nationwide. The cost of inaction arrives early.

choices.label: Borrow heavily for climate adaptation
choices.reaction: Flood barriers rise. Interest payments loom.

choices.label: Subsidise fossil fuel to keep prices down
choices.reaction: Pump prices fall. Climate goals slip further.

choices.label: Sue major polluters for disaster costs
choices.flavour: Make them pay for the damage.
choices.reaction: Courts clog with cases. Investors flee carbon-heavy sectors.

turns.prompt: Trade partners announce carbon border taxes. Your earlier choices determine who pays.

memory_callbacks.text: Partners remember every year you asked for more time.
The world now prices your exports like a high-carbon pariah.
The cap you enforced early left no slack when disaster struck.

choices.label: Emergency pivot — shut dirty plants within a year
Retaliate with tariffs of your own
choices.reaction: Shelves empty of imports. Nationalists cheer.

choices.label: Accept penalties and protect domestic jobs
choices.flavour: Pay the fine, keep the factories.
choices.reaction: Treasury wires payments abroad. Jobs stay — for now.

turns.prompt: Final reckoning. What balance did you strike between planet and paycheck?

choices.label: Lead on climate regardless of short-term cost
choices.reaction: Summits cite your name. Heavy industry scales down.

choices.label: Put economic growth first
choices.reaction: Payrolls swell. Weather grows stranger — and insurance premiums notice.

choices.label: Pursue a just transition with shared sacrifice
choices.reaction: The transition is just — on paper. Coal towns read the footnotes.
